name: Icelandic Three Grain Brown Bread
recipeInstructions:
This soda bread recipe comes from an Icelandic farmhouse. It's dark, delicious, and full of wholesome grains. It's also excellent toasted.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Grease two 9x5x3 inch loaf pans.
In a large bowl, cream together the brown sugar and butter.
In another bowl, mix the rolled oats, rye flour, whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, salt, and baking soda.
Add the creamed mixture and buttermilk to the dry ingredients. Mix until well blended.
Divide the mixture between the two pans and smooth the tops.
Bake for 1 hour or until a wooden skewer inserted in the center comes out clean.
Remove from the pans and cool on racks. Makes 2 loaves.
